name: jinko-sdk

metadata.name: jinko-sdk
metadata.summary: A Python package containing jinko_helpers, jinko_stats, and crabbit modules aimed to be used with Jinko.ai API

documentation:
# Jinko API Helpers

Python helpers function to ease use of Jinko's API

Jinko is an innovative SaaS and CaaS platform focused on trial simulation and design optimization. The Jinko API offers programmatic access to a wide range of functionalities, facilitating integration with various tools and enhancing collaboration.

## Usage

```sh
pip install jinko-sdk

# Or

poetry add jinko-sdk

```

```python
import jinko_helpers as jinko

# Connect to Jinko
jinko.initialize(
    projectId='016140de-1753-4133-8cbf-e67d9a399ec1',
    apiKey='50b5085e-3675-40c9-b65b-2aa8d0af101c'
)

# Check authentication
response = jinko.makeRequest('/app/v1/auth/check')

# Make a few requests
projectItem = jinko.makeRequest(
    '/app/v1/project-item/tr-EUsp-WjjI',
    method='GET',
).json()

```




Find out more at [Jinko Doc](https://doc.jinko.ai)

Read the "JSON VpopDesign" from a local file. The local counterpart of jinko.vpop.get_vpop_design_content.
Note that local file does not have "computationalModelId"

documentation: Stream a list of input items, given by either URL or file paths.

functions.name: crabbit.utils.merge_vpops
documentation: Merge a stream of vpops into one vpop, concatenating the patients.

functions.name: crabbit.utils.merge_vpop_designs
documentation:
Merge a stream of vpop designs into one, concatenating the marginals and correlations.
When VpopDesigns are attached to different models/versions, the LAST ONE in the list is kept.
Note: the resulting VpopDesign can become incompatible with the CM.

functions.name: crabbit.utils.merge_csv
documentation: Merge the CSV by concateating rows, corresponding to merged vpop trial results.

functions.name: jinko_helpers.trial.is_trial_running
documentation:
Checks if the trial is completed.

Args:
    trial_core_item_id (str): The CoreItemId of the trial.
    trial_snapshot_id (str): The snapshot ID of the trial.

Returns:
    bool: True if the trial is completed, False otherwise.

functions.name: jinko_helpers.trial.monitor_trial_until_completion
documentation:
Polls the Jinko API for the status of a trial and prints a progress view with completed task counts.

Args:
    trial_core_item_id (str): The CoreItemId of the trial.
    trial_snapshot_id (str): The snapshot ID of the trial.
    time_to_completion (int, optional): Maximum time to monitor the trial in seconds. If not provided, monitors indefinitely.
    retry_interval (int, optional): Interval in seconds between retries. Default is 5 seconds.

Returns:
    pd.DataFrame: A DataFrame summarizing the perArm data.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: If no 'perArmSummary' data is found in the response or if the trial does not complete in the specified time.

functions.name: jinko_helpers.trial.get_task_count
documentation:
Calculates the total and completed task counts from the given per-arm data.

Args:
    per_arm_data (dict): A dictionary where each key is an arm identifier, and
                         its value is another dictionary containing counts of tasks
                         with keys 'countPending', 'countError', and 'countSuccess'.

Returns:
    dict: A dictionary containing 'total_tasks' which is the sum of pending, error,
          and success counts, and 'completed_tasks' which is the sum of error and success counts.

functions.name: jinko_helpers.vpop.get_vpop_content
documentation:
Retrieves the JSON data of a VPOP given its SID.

Args:
    vpop_sid (str): The SID of the Vpop.

Returns:
    Vpop: The VPOP data containing coreVersion, metadata and patients
    None: If an HTTP error occurs during the request.

functions.name: jinko_helpers.vpop.get_vpop_design_content
documentation:
Retrieves the JSON data of a VpopDesign given its SID.

Args:
    vpop_design_sid (str): The SID of the VpopDesign.

Returns:
    VpopDesignWithModel: The VpopGVpopDesignWithModelenerator data
    None: If an HTTP error occurs during the request.

functions.name: jinko_helpers.calibration.get_calib_status

Retrieves the calibration status for a given calibration core ID.

Args:

Returns:

modules.name: jinko_helpers.jinko_helpers
documentation:
This modules provides a set of helper functions for Jinko API

- configure authentication (jinko.initialize)
- check authentication (jinko.checkAuthentication)
- retrieve a ProjectItem (jinko.getProjectItem)
- retrieve the CoreItemId of a ProjectItem (jinko.getCoreItemId)
- make HTTP requests (jinko.makeRequest)

functions.name: jinko_helpers.jinko_helpers.encodeCustomHeaders
documentation:
Encodes and prepares custom headers for the Jinko API.

Args:
    custom_data (dict): Dictionary containing 'description', 'folder_id', 'name', 'version_name', 'output_format', 'input_format'

Returns:
    dict: Dictionary containing encoded and formatted headers.

functions.name: jinko_helpers.jinko_helpers.makeRequest
documentation:
Makes an HTTP request to the Jinko API.

Args:
    path (str): HTTP path
    method (str, optional): HTTP method. Defaults to 'GET'
    params (Dict, optional): Dictionary, list of tuples or bytes to send in the query string. Defaults to None
    json (Any, optional): input payload as JSON. Defaults to None
    csv_data (str, optional): input payload as a CSV formatted string. Defaults to None
    options (MakeRequestOptions, optional): additional options. Defaults to None
    data: (Any, optional): raw input payload. Defaults to None
Returns:
    Response: HTTP response object

Raises:
    requests.exceptions.HTTPError: if HTTP status code is not 200

Examples:
    response = makeRequest('/app/v1/auth/check')

    projectItem = makeRequest(
        '/app/v1/project-item/tr-EUsp-WjjI',
        method='GET',
    ).json()

    # pass query parameters
    projectItem = makeRequest(
        '/app/v1/project-item',
        params={
          "name": "Example",
          "type": ["ComputationalModel", "Trial"],
        },
        method='GET',
    ).json()

    # receive data in CSV format
    response = makeRequest('/core/v2/vpop_manager/vpop/9c9c0bc5-f447-4745-b5eb-41b18e5eb900',
        options={
            'output_format': 'text/csv'
        }
    )

    # send data in CSV format
    response = makeRequest('/core/v2/vpop_manager/vpop', method='POST',
        data="....",
        options={
            'input_format': 'text/csv'
        }
    )

functions.name: jinko_helpers.jinko_helpers.nextPage
documentation:
Retrieves the next page of a response

Args:
    lastResponse (Response): HTTP response object to retrieve next page for

Returns:
    Response|None: HTTP response object for next page or None if there is no next page

Raises:
    Exception: if HTTP status code is not 200

Examples:
    response = makeRequest('/app/v1/project-item')
    response = nextPage(response)

functions.name: jinko_helpers.jinko_helpers.fetchAllJson
documentation:
Makes a GET HTTP request and retrieve all pages of a paginated response as json

Args:
    path (str): HTTP path

Returns:
    Response: HTTP response object

Raises:
    Exception: if HTTP status code is not 200

Examples:
    trials = fetchAllJson('/app/v1/project-item/?type=Trial')

functions.name: jinko_helpers.jinko_helpers.checkAuthentication
documentation:
Checks authentication

Returns:
    bool: whether or not authentication was successful

Raises:
    Exception: if HTTP status code is not one of [200, 401]

Examples:
    if not jinko.checkAuthentication():
        print('Authentication failed')

functions.name: jinko_helpers.jinko_helpers.initialize
documentation:
Configures the connection to Jinko API and checks authentication

Args:
    projectId (str | None, optional): project Id. Defaults to None
        If None, fallbacks to JINKO_PROJECT_ID environment variable
        If environment variable is not set, you will be asked for it interactively
    apiKey (str | None, optional): API key value. Defaults to None
        If None, fallbacks to JINKO_API_KEY environment variable
        If environment variable is not set, you will be asked for it interactively
    baseUrl (str | None, optional): root url to reach Jinko API. Defaults to None
        If None, fallbacks to JINKO_BASE_URL environment variable
        If environment variable is not set, fallbacks to 'https://api.jinko.ai'
    jinkoUrl (str | None, optional): root url to reach Jinko front-end. Defaults to None
        If None, fallbacks to JINKO_URL environment variable
        If environment variable is not set, fallbacks to 'https://jinko.ai'

Raises:
    Exception: if API key is empty
    Exception: if Project Id is empty
    Exception: if authentication is invalid

Examples:
    jinko.initialize()

    jinko.initialize(
        '016140de-1753-4133-8cbf-e67d9a399ec1',
        apiKey='50b5085e-3675-40c9-b65b-2aa8d0af101c'
    )

    jinko.initialize(
        baseUrl='http://localhost:8000',
        jinkoUrl='http://localhost:3000'
    )

functions.name: jinko_helpers.jinko_helpers.getProjectItem
documentation:
Retrieves a single ProjectItem from its short Id
and optionally its revision number

Args:
    shortId (str): short Id of the ProjectItem
    revision (int | None, optional): revision number. Defaults to None

Returns:
    dict: ProjectItem

Raises:
    Exception: if HTTP status code is not 200

Examples:
    projectItem = jinko.getProjectItem('tr-EUsp-WjjI')

    projectItem = jinko.getProjectItem('tr-EUsp-WjjI', 1)

functions.name: jinko_helpers.jinko_helpers.getCoreItemId
documentation:
Retrieves the CoreItemId corresponding to a ProjectItem

Args:
    shortId (str): short Id of the ProjectItem
    revision (int | None, optional): revision number. Defaults to None

Returns:
    CoreItemId: corresponding CoreItemId

Raises:
    Exception: if HTTP status code is not 200
    Exception: if this type of ProjectItem has no CoreItemId

Examples:
    id = jinko.getCoreItemId('tr-EUsp-WjjI')

    id = jinko.getCoreItemId('tr-EUsp-WjjI', 1)

functions.name: jinko_helpers.jinko_helpers.dataTableToSQLite
documentation:
Converts a CSV file to an SQLite database and encodes it in base64.

Args:
    data_table_file_path (str): The path to the CSV file.

Returns:
    str: The encoded SQLite database.

Raises:
    FileNotFoundError: If the CSV file does not exist.

Examples:
    >>> datTableToSQLite('path/to/data.csv')
    'encoded_data_table'

functions.name: jinko_helpers.jinko_helpers.getProjectItemInfoFromResponse
documentation:
Retrieves the information contains in the "X-jinko-project-item"
header of the response

Args:
    response (Response): HTTP response object

Returns:
    ProjectItemInfoFromResponse | None: ProjectItem informations or None if header does not exist

Raises:
    Exception: if HTTP status code is not 200

Examples:
  >>> response = jinko.makeRequest(
  ...     path="/core/v2/model_manager/jinko_model",
  ...     method="POST",
  ...     json={"model": model, "solvingOptions": solving_options},
  ... )
  >>> jinko.getProjectItemInfoFromResponse(response)
  {"sid": "cm-pKGA-7r3O", "kind": "ComputationalModel", "coreItemId": {"id": "be812bcc-978e-4fe1-b8af-8fb521888718", "snapshotId": "ce2b76f6-07dd-47c6-9700-c70ce44f0507"}, "revision": 5}

functions.name: jinko_helpers.jinko_helpers.getProjectItemUrlFromSid
documentation:
Retrieves the URL of a ProjectItem based on its SID.

Args:
    sid (str): The SID of the ProjectItem.

Returns:
    str: The URL of the ProjectItem.

functions.name: jinko_helpers.jinko_helpers.getProjectItemUrlFromResponse
documentation:
Retrieves the URL of a ProjectItem from an HTTP response object.

Args:
    response (Response): HTTP response object.

Returns:
    str: The URL of the ProjectItem.

Raises:
    Exception: if the "X-jinko-project-item" header is not present in the response.

functions.name: jinko_helpers.jinko_helpers.getProjectItemUrlByCoreItemId
documentation:
Retrieves the URL of a ProjectItem based on its CoreItemId.
Args:
    coreItemId (str): The CoreItemId of the ProjectItem.
Returns:
    str: The URL of the ProjectItem.
Raises:
    requests.exceptions.RequestException: If there is an error making the request.
Examples:
    >>> getProjectItemUrlByCoreItemId("123456789")
    'https://jinko.ai/foo'

functions.name: jinko_helpers.jinko_helpers.is_interactive
documentation: Check if the environment supports interactive plot display (like Jupyter or IPython).

functions.name: jinko_helpers.jinko_helpers.show_plot_conditionally
documentation: Show the plot if in an interactive environment, otherwise save it.

classes.name: jinko_helpers.jinko_helpers.CoreItemId
documentation:
Represents the CoreItem identifier.

Attributes:
    id (str): The unique identifier of the CoreItem.
    snapshotId (str): Identifies a specific version of the CoreItem.

classes.name: jinko_helpers.jinko_helpers.MakeRequestOptions
documentation:
Additional options to use when making a request to the Jinko API.

Attributes:
    name (str): Name to use when creating/updating a ProjectItem (Modeling & Simulation).
    description (str): Description to use when creating/updating a ProjectItem (Modeling & Simulation).
    folder (str): Id of the destination folder to use when creating/updating a ProjectItem (Modeling & Simulation).
    version_name (str): Name of the new version when creating/updating a ProjectItem (Modeling & Simulation).
    input_format (str): Content type of the input payload.
    output_format (str): Expected content type of the response payload (may be ignored by server if not supported).

classes.name: jinko_helpers.jinko_helpers.ProjectItemInfoFromResponse
documentation:
Informations contained in the "X-jinko-project-item" header returned
by the Jinko API when creating/updating a ProjectItem (Modeling & Simulation).

Attributes:
    sid (str): Short Id of the ProjectItem.
    description (str): Type of the ProjectItem.
    coreItemId (CoreItemId): CoreItemId of the ProjectItem.
    revision (int): Revision number of the ProjectItem.

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.CalibrationOptions.numberOfIterations
documentation: When this number of iterations is reached, the calibration stops.

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.CalibrationOptions.populationSize
documentation: Size of the virtual population drawn at each iteration.

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.CalibrationOptions.seed
documentation: An arbitrary integer between 0 and 2^32-1 to initialize the random number generation.

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.CalibrationOptions.thresholdWeightedScore
documentation: The weighted score is the averaged sum of scores, for a given patient. This quantity is maximized during the optimization process. As soon as a patient exceeds this threshold weighted score, the calibration stops.

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.CalibrationParameter.maxBound

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.CalibrationParameter.mean

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.CalibrationParameter.minBound

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.CalibrationParameter.std

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.Mapping7.operation
documentation: Operation
Tabulate a table: creates columns _columnWithNames_ and _columnWithValues_ containing names and values from the columns in _columns_

classes.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.Mapping10

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.Mapping10.operation
documentation: Operation
Apply (binary) _function_ on _originColumn1_ and _originColumn2_ (infix: _origColumn1_ _function_ _origColumn2_) and store result in new column _destinationColumn_

classes.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.Mapping13

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.Mapping13.operation
documentation: Operation
Delete a column

documentation: Optional (defaults to 1). Scale σ. If x is the variable, we introduce the change of variable x'=x/σ and the initial Ode dx/dt=f(x,t) becomes dx'/dt=σ*f(σx',t) such that the variable we actually solve for, x', has the order of magnitude of x/σ

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.Specifics2.tolerance

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.TimeTrigger3.until

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.Specifics3.conditionTrigger

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.Specifics3.priority

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.Specifics3.record
documentation: Optional, defaults to false. If true, the state before and after the event is recorded in the ODE solution, adding 2 timepoints to the results

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.Specifics5.tolerance

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.Specifics7.boundaryCondition
documentation: Optional, defaults to false. When true, the amount of the species is not determined by any reaction. See the corresponding SBML documentation

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.Specifics7.compartment
documentation: Compartment where the species lives

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.Specifics7.constant
documentation: Optional, defaults to false. If set to true, the species amount remains constant over time, it will not be altered by any assignment formula, reaction, ODE or event. See the SBML documentation on the constant attribute

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.Specifics7.conversionFactor
documentation: Optional. Conversion factor is used to multiply all reaction rates to define the dynamics of this species. See 4.11.7 of the SBML L3V2 specifications

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.Specifics7.initialCondition
documentation: Initial condition (or formula) of the species

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.Specifics7.molarMass
documentation: Optional. The molar mass is used to convert the Species to the extentUnits when needed (see Unit)

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.Specifics7.scale
documentation: Optional (defaults to 1). Scale σ. If x is the variable, we introduce the change of variable x'=x/σ and the initial Ode dx/dt=f(x,t) becomes dx'/dt=σ*f(σx',t) such that the variable we actually solve for, x', has the order of magnitude of x/σ

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.Specifics7.tolerance
documentation: Optional (defaults to 1). The tolerance of the scaled variable (in dimensionless units). This value is multiplied by the model-wide tolAbs to get the actual absolute tolerance passed to the solver

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.Specifics7.unit
documentation: Unit used for the species initial condition. If the initial condition is a formula, the unit can be inferred and is therefore optional. Note that species used in formulas may be converted, meaning that they will be converted in extentUnits (defaults to mol) when used in a formula. See help center - Units for a list of available units and prefixes and more details on the conversions

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingTimes.tMax
documentation: Final time

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingTimes.tMin
documentation: Initial time

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingTimes.tStep
documentation: Step duration
An implicit solving time span

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingOptions.allowVaryingStoichiometry
documentation: Set to true if you need to allow for stoichiometric coefficients varying in time (not recommended but might be needed for SBML models allowing this feature)

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingOptions.discontinuityEvents
documentation: Adds auxiliary root-based events to help the ODE solver detect time-discontinuities in the right hand side (RHS). This is useful for time-adaptive solvers such as BDF to avoid missing short time-span variations in the system's RHS such as short term injections encoded via a time varying parameter

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingOptions.evaluator
documentation: Whether you use the LLVM optimized or the Native Haskell solver

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingOptions.extentUnits
documentation: The unit the species are converted to. See our unit syntax guide

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingOptions.inlineLimit
documentation: A number to control the maximum size (number of nodes) of expression trees that will be inlined before solving: 0 means there is no inlining, 1 means that we inline constants, etc...

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingOptions.maxEvents
documentation: Maximum number of root-based event occurrences

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingOptions.mutePhenomena
documentation: Phenomena to be muted

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingOptions.muteVariables
documentation: Variables to be muted

documentation: The initial step of the solver

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingOptions.odeSolverRelativeTolerance
documentation: The solver relative error tolerance

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingOptions.outputParameters

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingOptions.outputRates
documentation: Rates to output in the results files

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingOptions.outputVariables
documentation: Variables to output in the results files

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingOptions.scoringMode
documentation: Use ScoringV2, other modes are now deprecated

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingOptions.solver
documentation: The numerical solving method used

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingOptions.solvingTimes
documentation: Each entry in this array will be either an explicit time or a regularly spaced time span. Those will determine the time points returned in the results. They are different from the time points used internally for solving

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.Method1.boltzmannConstant
documentation: The probability of taking a step from a state of energy Ea to one of energy Eb where Eb > Ea is exp((Ea - Eb) / kT), defaults to 1e-3

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.Method1.itersFixedTemperature
documentation: Number of iterations for each temperature

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.Method1.numIterations
documentation: Total number of iterations of simulated annealing, must be >= itersFixedTemperature

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.Method1.replacementRate
documentation: Proportion of samples to swap at each iteration

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SubsamplingOptions.numSamples
documentation: How many patients do we want to subsample from the initial trial?

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SubsamplingOptions.seed
documentation: A seed to initialize the random number generator, ensures reproducibility of results

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingOptionsWithMetadata.allowVaryingStoichiometry
documentation: Set to true if you need to allow for stoichiometric coefficients varying in time (not recommended but might be needed for SBML models allowing this feature)

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingOptionsWithMetadata.discontinuityEvents
documentation: Adds auxiliary root-based events to help the ODE solver detect time-discontinuities in the right hand side (RHS). This is useful for time-adaptive solvers such as BDF to avoid missing short time-span variations in the system's RHS such as short term injections encoded via a time varying parameter

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingOptionsWithMetadata.evaluator
documentation: Whether you use the LLVM optimized or the Native Haskell solver

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingOptionsWithMetadata.extentUnits
documentation: The unit the species are converted to. See our unit syntax guide

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingOptionsWithMetadata.inlineLimit
documentation: A number to control the maximum size (number of nodes) of expression trees that will be inlined before solving: 0 means there is no inlining, 1 means that we inline constants, etc...

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingOptionsWithMetadata.maxEvents
documentation: Maximum number of root-based event occurrences

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingOptionsWithMetadata.mutePhenomena
documentation: Phenomena to be muted

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingOptionsWithMetadata.muteVariables
documentation: Variables to be muted

documentation: The initial step of the solver

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingOptionsWithMetadata.odeSolverRelativeTolerance
documentation: The solver relative error tolerance

documentation: Parameters to output in the results files

documentation: Rates to output in the results files

documentation: Variables to output in the results files

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingOptionsWithMetadata.scoringMode
documentation: Use ScoringV2, other modes are now deprecated

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingOptionsWithMetadata.solver

class_variables.name: jinko_helpers.types.api_types_dict.SolvingOptionsWithMetadata.solvingTimes
documentation: Each entry in this array will be either an explicit time or a regularly spaced time span. Those will determine the time points returned in the results. They are different from the time points used internally for solving
